free_text: Evaluated activation cross sections of longer-lived
radionuclides produced by deuteron-induced reactions
on natural copper
MGC-20E cyclotron
CGR-560 cyclotron
Cyclone-110 cyclotron
High purity (>99.5%) natural Cu foils from Goodfellow,
thicknesses: 9.95, 10.2 and 47.45 micron.
Several stacks were irradiated.
Each stack contained other high purity metallic foils
such as Al, Ti, Ni, Mo, Zn, W that allowed accurate
assessment of the beam intensity and the energy
degradation along the stacks and a cross-check of the
measured cross sections.
Primary energies of the deuteron beams:
measurements started: 1-44 h after the end of
bombardment and continued for several days.
Irradiations were done in a Faraday-cup as target
holder.
Nondestructive measurement without chemical separation.
Energy degradaton in the stacks was calculated by using
the stopping power data and polynomial approximation of
Andersen and Ziegler.
Decay data were taken from the nuclear decay database
of Lund University, Sweden.
Corrections to the beam current and to the energy
scale were based on re-measurements of the cross
sections of monitor reaction and on comparison with
the recommended data.
Chapter 4
The uncertainty
* in the primary beam energy: 0.3 MeV,
* in the mean energy in the last foil: 0.7 MeV
taking into account cumulative effects of the
possible variations in incident energy and
number of target atoms.
The following individual uncertainties are
included in the propagated error:
* number of target nuclei
including target non-uniformities 5%,
* incident particle intensity 4-6%,
* detection efficiency depending on the energy
of the gamma-photon 3-7%,
* determination of the peak areas
including statistical errors 1-9%,
* abundance of the gamma rays analysed 1-5%.
The total average resulting uncertainties: 8-15%,
obtained as square root of the sum of square of the
linearly contributing sources.
The measured experimental data were compared to
those found in the literature.
On the basis of the new experimental cross
sections and selected data taken from the literature
integral yields were calculated and plotted in Fig. 9
of the reference.
